friction_items.description: UNCHANGED from Phase 15a. src/research_os/tools/actions/audit/audit.py (~line 1758) still hard-codes the single 'humanities_mode' branch and still ignores step_summary.yaml's step_intent / figure_required contract. Confirmed empirically: dropped step_summary.yaml with step_intent=proof + figure_required=false into workspace/01_proof, ran tool_audit(scope=step, dimension=completeness) — gate still blocks with 'No figure produced — every step MUST emit at least one focal figure'. The proof_verification_workflow protocol still promises a contract that the audit gate refuses to honor. tool_audit, tool_synthesize, and the .audit_findings.jsonl ledger all faithfully propagate the same false-positive block. Phase 9/10/14 did not touch this code path. The single highest-leverage maintainer-perspective fix from the baseline is still unfixed.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline: add _load_step_summary(step_dir) in audit.py, read step_summary['step_intent'] / step_summary['figure_required'], and skip the focal-figure blocker (downgrade to warning) when intent in {proof, apparatus, plan, ground, synth} OR figure_required is False. Better: register the override via PackRegistration.completeness_overrides so adding a new pack does not require editing audit.py. This is the v2.0.0 release-blocker for the theory_math story to work end-to-end.

friction_items.description: UNCHANGED from Phase 15a. proof_verification_workflow.declare_step_contract step still tells the researcher to use verdict IMPORTED_AS_CITED for cited lemmas. src/research_os/tools/actions/audit/step_literature.py line 47 still hard-codes the verdict regex as (AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|DEFERRED). IMPORTED_AS_CITED is not in the enum, never matched, silently dropped. Empirically: ran tool_audit(scope=step, dimension=literature) on the proof step — verdict_roll_up reports the 4 legacy verdicts only. No new preflight check exists that greps every protocol body for r'verdict\s+[A-Z_]+' and asserts membership. Same broken-as-shipped state as baseline.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. (1) Either add IMPORTED_AS_CITED to the regex + roll-up dict (one-line fix) and exempt it from downstream discussion-coverage requirements, or auto-waive the literature audit when step_intent=proof. (2) Add a preflight check: walk every protocol YAML for r'verdict\s+[A-Z_]+', cross-check against the audit's enum, fail preflight on drift.

friction_items.description: UNCHANGED from Phase 15a. proof_verification_workflow.yaml step formal_check (line 137) still reads 'Run tool_theory_lean_check / tool_theory_coq_check.' — the actual registered tool names are tool_theory_math_lean_check / tool_theory_math_coq_check. No aliases added in src/research_os/server/aliases.py for these. An AI agent literally following the protocol prose hits 'tool not found'. The aliases.py file shows Phase 9/14 hard-removed 21 first-wave consolidation aliases but did not add the two needed compat aliases for the stale protocol prose. Preflight still does not scan protocol bodies for tool_* substrings. Two recurring classes of bug (tool-rename-without-prose-update, verdict-enum-without-preflight) ship together.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Either fix the protocol text or add the two aliases, AND extend scripts/preflight.py with a static check that scans every protocol's description / steps body for r'tool_[a-z_]+' and asserts each is a live tool. Without static enforcement this class of drift will keep recurring on every rename across every minor.

friction_items.description: UNCHANGED from Phase 15a. tool_theory_math_lean_check, when Lean is not installed, still returns {status: success, data: {status: warning, hint_path: ..., message: 'Lean 4 not installed; wrote install hint.'}}. Outer envelope lies; inner status quietly degrades. No tool_reliability(operation=log_event) emission, nothing in the .audit_findings.jsonl ledger. Phase 9 consolidated tool_reliability_log_event + tool_reliability_report into the single tool_reliability(operation=...) tool — that part is genuinely better — but the theory pack tools still never CALL it on missing-binary paths. A maintainer reviewing 'why are proof workflows getting stuck' across many sessions still has no telemetry trail for pack-tool unavailability. The reliability story is half-shipped: API consolidated, producers not wired.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline + adjusted for new tool surface. In research_os_theory_math/tools.py, on shutil.which()==None: (1) call tool_reliability(operation=log_event, kind='external_dep_missing', binary='lean') before returning; (2) return {status: 'unavailable', reason: 'lean_missing'} at the OUTER envelope, not the inner 'warning' shape. Same fix for tool_theory_math_coq_check. Add the theory pack tools to whatever writer feeds .audit_findings.jsonl so dashboard reliability tiles see them.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. sys_packs_installed shows humanities=1.9.3, theory_math=1.9.3, wet_lab=1.9.3, engineering=1.9.3, qualitative=1.11.0, core=2.0.0-dev. No scripts/sync_pack_versions.py was added. No preflight check 'all in-tree pack versions match core OR carry __version_pin__'. A v2.0.0 release that ships pack version 1.9.3 alongside core 2.0.0 will produce confusing sys_packs_installed output and force every external pack author to guess what version policy upstream actually follows. Severity downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM only because Phase 9/10/14 demonstrated the team can ship a major; doing so without the sync script is annoying but not broken.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Add scripts/sync_pack_versions.py that walks src/research_os_*/__init__.py + src/research_os_*/protocols/**/*.yaml and rewrites version: / __version__ to core version (or to a per-pack __version_pin__ override). Invoke from docs/RELEASING.md. Add a preflight check enforcing the policy.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. src/research_os_theory_math/router_entries.py for theory_math/method/proof_strategy_selection still injects graph-theory-specific triggers ['maximum degree', 'minimum degree', 'graph coloring', 'planar graph', 'sub-cubic', 'subcubic']. Empirically: tool_route('Prove that every planar graph with maximum degree 3 is 4-colorable') now correctly routes to theory_math/method/proof_strategy_selection (good), BUT it does so via matched_triggers=['maximum degree', 'planar graph'] (band-aid path, not principled routing). The biology DEG trigger in src/research_os/protocols/_router_index.yaml lines 364-367 is still unscoped — 'differential expression', 'DEG analysis', 'DEG list', 'DEGs' will still false-positive on any graph-theory prompt mentioning 'degree'. Severity stays MEDIUM because the routing WORKS, but the maintainer-debt of carrying domain-specific keywords inside generic protocol triggers grows monotonically as new fixtures arrive.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. (1) Scope DEG triggers to require a co-occurring biology context word (expression / RNA-seq / transcriptomics). (2) Move graph-theory triggers out of proof_strategy_selection into a dedicated graph_theory/* sub-pack. (3) Add preflight check: no protocol's triggers list should contain phrases that overlap with another pack's domain vocabulary.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. src/research_os/protocols/_router_index.yaml carries core triggers; src/research_os_theory_math/router_entries.py carries pack triggers via THEORY_MATH_ROUTER_ENTRIES dict. CLAUDE.md still instructs maintainers to update _router_index.yaml only. Pack registration silently bypasses that channel. There is no tool_router_dump for maintainers to see the merged map. Phase 9/10/14 did not unify or document the split. As external packs become the v2.0.0 PyPI plugin story, this gap multiplies — every plugin author will independently rediscover that router_entries.py is the way and CLAUDE.md is wrong for packs.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Either (a) update CLAUDE.md + PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md to document the dual-channel rule and the merge order explicitly, or (b) load pack router entries into the same in-memory _router_index map at pack-registration time AND expose tool_router_dump / sys_router_dump that prints the merged routing table.

friction_items.description: Still present though improved. The Phase 15a baseline flagged that pack-only tools (slurm, cytoscape, lean, coq, snakemake, nextflow, redcap, synapse) should be hidden behind active-pack detection so they do not pollute list_tools output for projects that have no SLURM cluster / no Cytoscape session / no Lean install. Phase 9/10/14 reduced the surface 344 → 146 (huge win) but pack-domain tools are still always-visible: of the 146 live tools, 23 belong to pack-domain categories (theory_math: 3, slurm: 7+, snakemake: 2, nextflow: 1, cytoscape: 1, redcap: 1, synapse: 1, humanities: 4, qualitative: 3, engineering: 3, wet_lab: 3). For the graph-theory proof fixture, only 3 of these (tool_theory_math_*) are usable; the other 20 are noise. sys_active_tools(protocol_name=...) does the scoping perfectly downstream, but the top-level list_tools that an MCP client sees on connect still shows all 146.
friction_items.suggested_fix: On sys_boot's first run for a project, persist the detected packs (already happens via detector). On the MCP list_tools call, filter TOOL_DEFINITIONS by pack ∈ (core ∪ active_packs). Tools from inactive packs become 'discoverable on demand' via sys_semantic_tool_search but do not bloat the default surface. Target default surface for an empirical-only project < 125 tools; for a theory-only project < 110 tools.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. No tests/unit/test_theory_math_pack_unavailable_paths.py was added in Phase 9-14. The lean_check / coq_check missing-binary path that produces the misleading 'success/warning' envelope (HIGH item above) is not exercised by any unit test. A future refactor of workspace/proof/lean_install_hint.md path would silently break the install-hint location with zero test coverage.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Add tests/unit/test_theory_math_pack_unavailable_paths.py: monkeypatch shutil.which to return None for lean/coq, dispatch the tools, assert envelope.status == 'unavailable' (after the HIGH-severity envelope fix above), assert hint file exists at the documented path.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. theory_math/proof/lemma_library protocol still does not specify WHERE lemmas land (workspace/lemmas/lemma_library.yaml? workspace/lemmas/<slug>.md?), what format (markdown? YAML schema?), or how versioning is enforced. theorem_dependency_graph.reconcile_informal step still references lemma_library.md as a seed file with no schema validation. A maintainer cannot write a fixture test 'after lemma_library runs, theorem_dependency_graph reads it' because the contract is unspecified.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Pin lemma_library output to workspace/lemmas/lemma_library.yaml with an explicit schema. Add a fixture test that round-trips a lemma through both protocols.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. tool_theory_math_dep_graph description still says 'approximate (lexical, not type-checked) but accurate enough to spot which lemmas a result transitively depends on'. The lexical extractor still misses tactic-mediated deps (exact?, apply?, simp sets, open Foo, anonymous have). lean --print-deps integration still not added.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Rewrite the description to honestly call out the lower-bound semantics, or integrate lean --print-deps behind a feature flag.

friction_items.description: Still present from baseline. No docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md exists. A maintainer wanting to author a new pack still has to read CLAUDE.md (incomplete for packs), docs/PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md (doesn't cover pack registration), src/research_os/plugins/pack_api.py, and reverse-engineer research_os_theory_math as a worked example. v2.0.0's PyPI plugin promise needs this doc to be real.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Same as baseline. Add docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md covering directory layout, PackRegistration fields with examples, router_entries.py shape, detector heuristics, paper_sections override, tool layout, test fixtures.

convoluted_tools: tool_theory_math_lean_check / tool_theory_math_coq_check — name still carries redundant 'math' infix. The pack is theory_math; the tools are pack-scoped already. tool_theory_lean_check + tool_theory_coq_check would be 4 chars shorter and clearer in tool_route output AND would match the protocol prose that nobody has fixed (see HIGH item above)

sys_protocol_get vs sys_protocol_list vs sys_protocol_history vs sys_protocol_log vs sys_protocol_next vs sys_protocol_validate AND tool_protocols_list (note plural) — 7 protocol-introspection tools with overlapping verbs. Phase 9 collapsed audit/dashboard/figure/search/plan/data/reliability families but did not touch the sys_protocol_* family. Same observation as baseline, less weight because consolidation elsewhere proved possible — making this an oversight rather than a structural problem.

sys_active_tools vs sys_tool_describe vs sys_semantic_tool_search vs tool_tools_list — 4 ways to interrogate the tool surface, none of them obviously the canonical entry point. A new maintainer must read all 4 short descriptions to figure out which to reach for. Consider consolidating into a single sys_tools(operation=list|describe|active|search) on the same pattern as tool_audit / tool_dashboard.
confusing_protocols: proof_verification_workflow.declare_step_contract — STILL refers to a contract (step_intent / figure_required) that the audit gate does not honor (see HIGH item 1) AND still references the verdict IMPORTED_AS_CITED that the audit enum does not accept (see HIGH item 2) AND still names tool_theory_lean_check / tool_theory_coq_check that are not registered (see HIGH item 3). Three live protocol-vs-code drift bugs in a single step. The protocol is a stress test of what gets caught by NO preflight check.
theory_math/proof/lemma_library — still ships without a specified output schema (see LOW item above)
theory_math/proof/theorem_dependency_graph — still depends on lemma_library.md (with no schema) AND on tool_theory_math_dep_graph (which honestly under-covers what its description claims)
improvements_vs_baseline: TOOL SURFACE 344 -> 146 (-57.6%). All 146 are now status=live; aliases (~50) silently redirect via _ALIASES dict without surfacing in list_tools. The biggest single source of maintainer cognitive load measured in Phase 15a is materially smaller.

AUDIT FAMILY consolidated 24 -> 3 (tool_audit + tool_audit_quality_full + tool_audit_findings). tool_audit takes (scope, dimension) tuple; old per-dimension names (tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_figure, etc.) are silent aliases that still work. Verified empirically — alias resolution succeeded.

DASHBOARD FAMILY consolidated 7 -> 1 (tool_dashboard with operation=create|story_*|reviewer_sim|test_*). FIGURE family consolidated 4 -> 1 (tool_figure with operation=palette|caption_synthesise|interactive_autogen|paper_autoembed). SEARCH 5 -> 1. PLAN 3 -> 1. DATA 3 -> 1. RELIABILITY 2 -> 1. LESSONS 5 -> 1. The pattern is consistent and clearly the result of a single deliberate phase rather than ad-hoc cleanup.

server.py REFACTORED 7499 lines (1 file) -> 8482 lines across 32 files in src/research_os/server/{entry,dispatch,aliases,tool_definitions/*,handlers/*}.py. Largest single file is now 579 lines (tool_definitions/meta.py). Greppability dramatically improved — 'where does tool_audit dispatch?' now answers itself via filename instead of needing scrolling through 7.5K lines.

TOOL DEFINITIONS NOW CARRY status + pack FIELDS as Phase 15b context promised. 146 / 146 tools carry both. Pack breakdown is honest: 123 core + 23 across 11 pack labels. A maintainer can finally answer 'what tools belong to which pack' without grepping registration code.
PROTOCOLS NOW CARRY scope_tags (domain, audience, workflow_shape). All 8 theory_math protocols carry the field. Phase 12 was clearly real.

sys_protocol_get DEFAULT FORMAT IS NOW 'summary' (was 'full'). Empirically: proof_verification_workflow summary = 3024 chars vs full = 9020 chars (3x shrink on the protocol I drove). This is huge for the AI-client perspective and is also a real maintenance win — protocol authors can keep adding rationale prose without bloating every single sys_protocol_get response.

MCP server INSTRUCTIONS field is now populated at handshake naming the boot sequence: sys_boot -> tool_route -> sys_protocol_get(format=summary) -> sys_active_tools. Phase 9 cross-cutting commitment delivered.

tool_route OUTPUT NOW INCLUDES recommended_action FIELD (e.g. 'sys_protocol_get(protocol_name=...)'). Removes the 'what do I do with this routing result' guesswork that the baseline flagged. why_matched also carries through cleanly.

sys_active_tools NOW SCOPES TO 13 TOOLS per protocol on average — verified for both proof_strategy_selection and proof_verification_workflow. Of the 146 tools in the global surface, an executing AI sees ~13 at any given moment. The cognitive-load story is now solidly on the right side of the line.

PHASE 14 DELETED THE FIRST-WAVE CONSOLIDATION ALIASES (tool_search_*, tool_plan_*, tool_ground_*, tool_claim_verify, tool_grounding_verify, tool_lessons_record/consult, sys_path_*, mem_methods_append, mem_decision_log, mem_hypothesis_update, mem_analysis_log) — Phase 15a's 'consolidation has net-added complexity because old names still ship' criticism was directly addressed. _REMOVED_TOOLS now gives a friendly error naming the canonical entry point.

AUDIT FINDINGS LEDGER (.audit_findings.jsonl) works end-to-end with stable UUIDv5 ids per finding, queryable via tool_audit_findings(operation=query). tool_synthesize correctly refuses synthesis when unresolved BLOCK findings remain in the ledger. The audit -> synthesis pipeline is well-knit.

NEVER-CALLED LIST SHRANK MASSIVELY. Of Phase 15a's 62 baseline never-called tool names, 49 (79%) are now absorbed into consolidated tools or hard-removed from the public surface. Only 13 (mostly pack-execution-only: slurm, R, Julia, notebook exec) remain.
remaining_recommendations: v2.1.0 PRIORITY 1: Fix the audit gate pack-blindness (HIGH item 1). Add _load_step_summary() to audit.py + register the per-pack override via PackRegistration.completeness_overrides. This is the single biggest blocker for the theory_math story to actually work end-to-end — the entire pack is structurally undermined by an audit gate that does not understand it. Same fix the baseline asked for; nothing about Phase 9-14 made it harder to do.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 2: Fix the three protocol-vs-code drifts in proof_verification_workflow.declare_step_contract (HIGH items 2+3): add IMPORTED_AS_CITED to the literature verdict enum + add tool_theory_lean_check / tool_theory_coq_check aliases. THEN add the two preflight checks that prevent recurrence: (a) every protocol body's tool_* substring is a live tool, (b) every verdict X referenced in any protocol body is a member of the consuming audit's enum. Without static enforcement this class of drift will keep recurring every minor.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 3: Wire pack-tool failure telemetry (HIGH item 4). Make tool_theory_math_lean_check / coq_check call tool_reliability(operation=log_event) on shutil.which failure AND return outer envelope status='unavailable' instead of lying as 'success'. The reliability tool already consolidated cleanly; the producers just need to be wired.
v2.1.0 PRIORITY 4: Add scripts/sync_pack_versions.py + preflight enforcement. Shipping packs at 1.9.3 alongside core 2.0.0 is sloppy and external pack authors will pattern-match the smell.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 5: Hide pack-only tools (slurm, snakemake, nextflow, cytoscape, redcap, synapse, R/Julia/notebook exec, lean/coq, plate-map / fault-tree renderers) behind active-pack detection at the list_tools layer. Target default surface < 125 tools for an empirical-only project, < 110 for a theory-only project. Tools remain reachable via sys_semantic_tool_search.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 6: Move graph-theory band-aid triggers OUT of theory_math/method/proof_strategy_selection (band-aid for v1.11.0 DEG false-positive) into a dedicated graph_theory/* sub-pack. Scope the biology DEG trigger to require a co-occurring biology context word. Add preflight check forbidding cross-pack vocab overlap in trigger lists.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 7: Add docs/PACK_AUTHORING.md. The v2.0.0 PyPI plugin story needs this. Currently external pack authors must reverse-engineer research_os_theory_math + read three CLAUDE.md sections + read PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md to figure out registration. Same recommendation as baseline.

v2.1.0 PRIORITY 8: Merge pack router_entries.py into the canonical _router_index at registration time + expose sys_router_dump for the merged view. CLAUDE.md vs reality drift is exactly the kind of thing that hurts external contributors.
v2.1.0 PRIORITY 9: Add tests/unit/test_theory_math_pack_unavailable_paths.py covering the missing-Lean / missing-Coq paths so the envelope fix from PRIORITY 3 cannot silently regress.
v2.1.0 PRIORITY 10: Specify lemma_library.yaml schema + path. Round-trip test through theorem_dependency_graph.
v2.1.0 PRIORITY 11: Honest description rewrite for tool_theory_math_dep_graph (lower-bound semantics). Optional: lean --print-deps integration behind a feature flag.

v2.1.0 NICE-TO-HAVE: Drop the redundant 'math' infix from tool_theory_math_{lean,coq,dep_graph}_* — they are pack-scoped already, the second 'math' is noise (and matches the prose in proof_verification_workflow that PRIORITY 2 will need to align anyway). Consolidate the sys_protocol_* family (7 tools) and the tool surface introspection cluster (sys_active_tools / sys_tool_describe / sys_semantic_tool_search / tool_tools_list) following the Phase 9 audit/dashboard pattern.
